en.extract:
The blue-capped rock thrush (Monticola cinclorhyncha) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae.


Description

The male has a blue head, chin and throat. The upper parts are blue and black. The rump and underparts are chestnut brown. There is a white patch on the wing that is visible during flight. The female bird is brown with a brown and white underside.


Distribution and breeding

The blue-capped rock thrush breeds in dense forests in the Himalayas and winters in the lowlands of southern India. During winter it is found primarily in the Western Ghats and Eastern Ghats of southern India.


Habits
Like thrushes, they fly up into trees and sit motionless when they are disturbed. It is a summer visitor in parts of Afghanistan and along the Himalayas from Pakistan to Arunachal Pradesh. In summer it is found in pine forests and hill slopes. In winter it is found in dense canopied forests.


References

The '''blue-capped rock thrush''' ('''''Monticola cinclorhyncha''''') is a species of bird in the family [[chat (bird)|Muscicapidae]].

==Description==
[[File:Blue-capped Rock Thrush.ogv|thumb|150px|left|Molem, Goa, India Nov 1997]]
The male has a blue head, chin and throat. The upper parts are blue and black. The rump and underparts are chestnut brown. There is a white patch on the wing that is visible during flight. The female bird is brown with a brown and white underside.<ref name=abc>{{cite book|last1=Ali|first1=Salim|title=The Book of Indian Birds|date=1996|publisher=Oxford University Press|location=India|page=284}}</ref>

==Distribution and breeding==
[[File:Blue-capped Rock Thrush - Monticola cinclorhyncha male in Yercaud, Tamil Nadu, India.jpg|thumb|Male [[Blue-capped rock thrush|Blue-capped Rock Thrush]] perched on ''[[Sterculia urens]]'', [[Yercaud]], Tamil Nadu, India]]
[[File:1J6A5501-Blue Capped Rock Thrush.jpg|thumb|Blue Capped Rock Thrush in [[Nepal]].]]
The blue-capped rock thrush breeds in dense forests in the [[Himalayas]] and winters in the lowlands of southern [[India]]. During winter it is found primarily in the [[Western Ghats]] and [[Eastern Ghats]] of southern India.<ref name="CollarBotW">{{cite journal |last1=Collar |first1=Nigel |title=Blue-capped Rock-Thrush (Monticola cinclorhyncha) |journal=Birds of the World |date=4 March 2020 |doi=10.2173/bow.bcrthr1.01 |url=https://birdsoftheworld-org.elibrary.calgarypubliclibrary.com/bow/species/bcrthr1/cur/introduction#fieldid |access-date=1 February 2024|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

==Habits==
Like thrushes, they fly up into trees and sit motionless when they are disturbed. It is a summer visitor in parts of Afghanistan and along the Himalayas from Pakistan to Arunachal Pradesh. In summer it is found in pine forests and hill slopes. In winter it is found in dense canopied forests.<ref>Rasmussen, P and Anderton, J. C. (2005) Birds of South Asia. The Ripley Guide. Vols 1 and 2. Smithsonian Institution and Lynx Edicions</ref>

zh.extract:
蓝头矶鸫（学名：Monticola cinclorhyncha）为鶲科矶鸫属的鸟类，是一种候鸟，俗名臭皮翠、白喉叽鸫、白喉石鸫、葫芦翠、虎皮翠。分布于西伯利亚、巴基斯坦、印度、东南亚各国、中国等地，主要生活于多岩山地的林间。该物种的模式产地在喜马拉雅山脉。


亚种
蓝头矶鸫普通亚种（学名：Monticola cinclorhyncha gularis），俗名蓝投头叽鸫百喉亚种。该物种的模式产地在天津。
